'File XXIX/2 Slavery in Kuwait' [3r] (5/296)

COPY EXTRACT No 585 of 1907
Koweit, 2nd Nov 07
To,
Political resident,

Sir,

I have the honour to report for your information and record that a slave named Faraj bin Said, holder of a manumission certificate from the Pol: Agent Bahrein, dated the 19th May 1907, appeared before me on the 27th ultimo and complained that his master, Khalifa-uz-Zaidi, had enti-
ced him here on the promise of a wife and that on the very day when he got him to Koweit he attempted to sell him at the shop of the dallal, Mawash.

2. On the evening of the 28th the matter was represent-
ed to Sheikh Jabir who said that he would make enquiries into the case and that, if the slave's account was true and he promised/ had been the property of a Bahrein subject and freed in Bahrein no one in Koweit would be allowed to molest him or sell him. But that if he was the property of a Kowe-
eit subject, the Sheikh contended that the Pol: Agent Bah-
rein had no right to free him.

3. I replied that, in that case it would be necessary for me to put the case up to the resident, Bushire, for orders and that I hoped that the Sheikh would issue such orders as would avoid opening up a theory and difficult question. In the meantime the slave was allowed to remain at the Agency for 3 days and fed at the expense of Government.

4. Sheikh Jabir, at his interview with me on the evening of the 30th, informed me that he had made enquiries into the case and found that the slave was from Bahrein and of the Bahrein owners; that he had told him that they had no right to bring and sell in Koweit slaves bearing manumission papers from the Pol: Agent Bahrein and that he had given orders that the slave was to be allowed to go free and that no one was to sell him.

I have etc,

S.G. KNOX.
